text:
The Paris Olympicsis in full swing with medals being handed out for events across the sporting spectrum.

Paris became the second city after London to host the Summer Games three times, 100 years on from the last time athletes descended on the French capital.

But ahead of Sunday’s closing ceremony, who will Paris be passing the Olympic torch on to?

Los Angeles will host the Games four years from now, following in London and now Paris’ footsteps as a three-time host city.

The City of Angels had originally competed against Paris to host the 2024 Games, but after several withdrawals, the two agreed to divvy up the duties.

Paris pushed to host in 2024, leaving Los Angeles with the responsibility of 2028.

LA28, as it’s been dubbed, will take place during the final two weeks of July - a shift from recent Games which have crept over into August.

No country has hosted more Summer Games than the US, with LA28 set to be the fifth time the event has been held Stateside and the first time since 1996.

In fact, the LA Games will be the ninth time the US has hosted the Olympics - both summer and winter.



Other host cities include St.Louis (1904 - summer), Lake Placid (1932 and 1980 - winter), Squaw Valley (1960 - winter), Atlanta (1996 - summer) and Salt Lake (2002 - winter).

Since the 2020 Games, the Summer Olympics have allowed up to six optional sports to be included alongside its ‘core’ events, such as athletics, swimming and gymnastics.

Making their debuts at the Games will be squash and flag football - a variant of American football.





While not the first time they’d have been involved, baseball, cricket and lacrosse have also been added to the roster - the latter two for the first time since 1900 and 1908 respectively.

The Games schedule is already mapped out all the way through to 2032.

Milan is due to hold the next edition of the Winter Games in 2026, whereas Brisbane has been named the host city for the Summer Games in 2032.

It will be the first time in three decades that Australia have hosted the Olympics and the third time overall, after Sydney 2000 and Melbourne 1956.

title: When is the next Olympic Games? All the details as America hosts 2028 event | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Amos Murphy
description: The US will host the Summer Games for a record-extending fifth time in 2028.
